metadata.description: Not used, but needed to pass arm-ttk test `Location-Should-Not-Be-Hardcoded`.  We instead use the `workspace-location` which is derived from the LA workspace
[concat('Region to deploy solution resources -- separate from location selection',parameters('location'))]
Workspace name for Log Analytics where Microsoft Sentinel is setup
resource group name where Microsoft Sentinel is setup
subscription id where Microsoft Sentinel is setup

connectorUiConfig.title: Microsoft Copilot
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The Microsoft Copilot logs connector in Microsoft Sentinel enables seamless ingestion of Copilot-generated activity logs from M365 Copilot and Security Copilot into Microsoft Sentinel for advanced threat detection, investigation and response. It collects telemetry from Microsoft Copilot services such as usage data and system responses and ingests into Microsoft Sentinel, allowing security teams to monitor for misuse, detect anomalies, and maintain compliance with organizational policies.

dataTypes.name: CopilotActivity
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CopilotActivity
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

customs.name: Tenant Permissions
customs.description: 'Security Administrator' or 'Global Administrator' on the workspace's tenant.

instructionSteps.title: Connect Microsoft Copilot audit logs to Microsoft Sentinel
instructionSteps.description: This connector uses the Office Management API to get your Microsoft Copilot audit logs. The logs will be stored and processed in your existing Microsoft Sentinel workspace. You can find the data in the **CopilotActivity** table.

connectorUiConfig.title: Microsoft Copilot
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The Microsoft Copilot logs connector in Microsoft Sentinel enables seamless ingestion of Copilot-generated activity logs from M365 Copilot and Security Copilot into Microsoft Sentinel for advanced threat detection, investigation and response. It collects telemetry from Microsoft Copilot services such as usage data and system responses and ingests into Microsoft Sentinel, allowing security teams to monitor for misuse, detect anomalies, and maintain compliance with organizational policies.

dataTypes.name: CopilotActivity
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CopilotActivity
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

customs.name: Tenant Permissions
customs.description: 'Security Administrator' or 'Global Administrator' on the workspace's tenant.

instructionSteps.title: Connect Microsoft Copilot audit logs to Microsoft Sentinel
instructionSteps.description: This connector uses the Office Management API to get your Microsoft Copilot audit logs. The logs will be stored and processed in your existing Microsoft Sentinel workspace. You can find the data in the **CopilotActivity** table.

properties.description:
Detects when file uploads are disabled in Copilot. Attackers often disable logging or file upload capabilities to avoid evidence collection and cover their tracks.
This rule identifies potential data exfiltration cover-up scenarios where security controls are being disabled.
properties.query:
CopilotActivity
| where RecordType == "DisableCopilotPlugin"
| extend Resource = parse_json(LLMEventData).Resource[0]
| extend Property = tostring(Resource.Property)
| extend Old = tostring(Resource.OriginalValue)
| extend New = tostring(Resource.NewValue)
| where Property == "FileUploads" and Old == "Enabled" and New == "Disabled"
| project TimeGenerated, ActorName, SrcIpAddr, Property, Old, New

properties.description: CopilotJailbreakAttempt_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.2

Detects jailbreak attempts in Copilot interactions where users are trying to bypass Copilot guardrails and security controls.
This rule identifies prompt injection and LLM abuse scenarios that could lead to initial access, credential access, or system impact.
properties.query:
CopilotActivity
| where RecordType == "CopilotInteraction"
| where LLMEventData has "JailbreakDetected"
| extend Data = parse_json(LLMEventData)
| extend Jailbreak = tostring(Data.Messages[0].JailbreakDetected)
| where Jailbreak == "true"
| project TimeGenerated, ActorName, AIModelName, Jailbreak

properties.description:
Detects when a normal user creates a Copilot plugin. This can be used to inject malicious prompts, tools, or data exfiltration paths.
This rule identifies potential persistence or privilege misuse scenarios where non-administrative users create plugins that could be leveraged for malicious purposes.
properties.displayName: Copilot - Plugin Created by Non-Admin User
properties.query:
CopilotActivity
| where RecordType == "CreateCopilotPlugin"
| where ActorUserType != "Admin"
| extend Data = parse_json(LLMEventData)
| extend Plugin = tostring(Data.Resource[0].Property)
| project TimeGenerated, ActorName, ActorUserId, SrcIpAddr, Plugin

properties.displayName: Copilot - Plugin Created by Non-Admin User

properties.description: CopilotPluginTampering_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.2

Detects when a user enables and disables Copilot plugins within a 5-minute window. This behavior often indicates probing for security controls or living-off-Copilot techniques.
This rule identifies discovery and defense evasion activities where users rapidly toggle plugin states, potentially testing security boundaries.
properties.displayName: Copilot - Plugin Tampering (Enable and Disable Within 5 Minutes)
properties.query:
CopilotActivity
| where RecordType in ("EnableCopilotPlugin","DisableCopilotPlugin")
| summarize
    Actions = make_set(RecordType),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
  by ActorName, SrcIpAddr
| where Actions has "EnableCopilotPlugin" and Actions has "DisableCopilotPlugin"
| where LastSeen - FirstSeen < 5m
| extend AccountCustomEntity = ActorName, IPCustomEntity = SrcIpAddr

properties.displayName: Copilot - Plugin Tampering (Enable and Disable Within 5 Minutes)

properties.description: CopilotExternalIPAccess_HuntingQueries Hunting Query with template version 3.0.2

properties.displayName: Copilot - Access From External IP Address
properties.query:
CopilotActivity
| where isnotempty(SrcIpAddr)
| where SrcIpAddr !startswith "10."
  and SrcIpAddr !startswith "172."
  and SrcIpAddr !startswith "192.168"
| project TimeGenerated, ActorName, ActorUserId, SrcIpAddr, RecordType

tags.name: description
tags.value:
Detects when Copilot is accessed from an external IP address outside the corporate network. This is very dangerous if an attacker is using Copilot to enumerate data.
This rule identifies potential account compromise scenarios where valid accounts are being used from unauthorized locations.

properties.displayName: Copilot - Access From External IP Address

properties.description: CopilotPluginReEnabled_HuntingQueries Hunting Query with template version 3.0.2

properties.displayName: Copilot - Plugin Enabled After Being Disabled
properties.query:
CopilotActivity
| where RecordType == "EnableCopilotPlugin"
| extend Old = extractjson("$.Resource[0].OriginalValue", LLMEventData)
| extend New = extractjson("$.Resource[0].NewValue", LLMEventData)
| where Old == "Disabled" and New == "Enabled"
| project TimeGenerated, ActorName, SrcIpAddr, Old, New

tags.name: description
tags.value:
Detects when a Copilot plugin is re-enabled after being previously disabled. This could indicate a possible attacker restoring their backdoor.
This rule identifies security control bypass scenarios where disabled plugins are reactivated, potentially indicating malicious activity or policy violations.

properties.displayName: Copilot - Plugin Enabled After Being Disabled

properties.description: @{workbookKey=MicrosoftCopilotActivityMonitoring; logoFileName=Copilot_logo.svg; description=Microsoft Copilot Activity Monitoring.; dataTypesDependencies=System.Object[]; dataConnectorsDependencies=System.Object[]; previewImagesFileNames=System.Object[]; version=1.0.0; title=Microsoft Copilot Activity Monitoring Workbook; templateRelativePath=MicrosoftCopilotActivityMonitoring.json; subtitle=; provider=Microsoft Sentinel Community}.description

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The Microsoft Copilot solution allows you to stream your Microsoft Copilot audit logs from M365 Copilot and Security Copilot into Microsoft Sentinel in order to track Copilot activities across your organization.

Underlying Microsoft Technologies used:

This solution takes a dependency on the following technologies, and some of these dependencies either may be in Preview state or might result in additional ingestion or operational costs:

  • Office Management API

Data Connectors: 1, Workbooks: 1, Analytic Rules: 4, Hunting Queries: 2

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
